info.title: dotnet-dump
info.description: Diagnostic dump collect and analyze tool

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --process-id
options.description: The process id to collect a memory dump.

options.name: --output
options.description:
The path where collected dumps should be written. Defaults to '.\dump_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dmp' on Windows and './core_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS'
on Linux where YYYYMMDD is Year/Month/Day and HHMMSS is Hour/Minute/Second. Otherwise, it is the full path and file name of the dump.

options.name: --type
options.description: The dump type determines the kinds of information that are collected from the process. There are several types: Full - The largest dump containing all memory including the module images. Heap - A large and relatively comprehensive dump containing module lists, thread lists, all stacks, exception information, handle information, and all memory except for mapped images. Mini - A small dump containing module lists, thread lists, exception information and all stacks. Triage - A small dump containing module lists, thread lists, exception information, all stacks and PII removed.

options.name: --name
options.description: The name of the process to collect a memory dump.

options.name: --diagnostic-port
options.description: The path to a diagnostic port to be used. Must be a runtime connect port.

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Starts an interactive shell with debugging commands to explore a dump

options.name: --command
options.description: Runs the command on start. Multiple instances of this parameter can be used in an invocation to chain commands. Commands will get run in the order that they are provided on the command line. If you want dotnet dump to exit after the commands, your last command should be 'exit'.

arguments.name: dump_path
arguments.description: Name of the dump file to analyze.

commands.name: ps
commands.description: Lists the dotnet processes that dumps can be collected from.
